content:
It roughly means that her songs, although popular in her homeland (that Wild Star thing), act as mere commodities among aristocracy 
Considering her story, she struggled to make a hard life by singing back in her homeland, so there might be exploit from their aristocracies for her and she’s indicating that in the sentence 
Don’t know if this explanation is clear enough to you

A proposal on a possible change of WoD name:
the EN name for Tulu’s SR wheel 青石王座 currently is “Blue Stone Throne”. In is his deepdive/awakener story the throne is described as 青金石, which directly refers to Lapis lazuli/Lazurite, a kind of stone usually appears to be blue.
However, in the original story “Call of Cthulhu” those stones representing R’lyeh are described as “soapy greenish-black stone/slimy green stone”, which is…green. The story of Tulu’s SR wheel says 青藻缠绕的轨迹…青色王座, so here 青should be the color of seaweeds, which is again closer to green. The CGs involving Tulu and his throne also show a green atmosphere rather than blue.
Anyway describing 青石 as “blue stones” seems inaccurate here. Considering there are some Lazurite pieces appear to be greenish, maybe we can change this name as “Lazurite Throne”…? ( “Greenish Stone Throne” or something like that seems to lose the reference to 青金石 on the other hand)
Any thoughts?
makes sense to me, I think Lazurite Throne makes sense
if nothing else it does seem to be clearly green and not blue so at minimum it should be something more reminiscent of either green-ness or the sea
Green Stone Throne sounds a bit odd...Lazurite sounds the best to me
but since 青藻 is algae/seaweed, maybe Sea Stone Throne or Azure Throne?

Azure Throne sounds fine for me too
Sea Stone Throne sounds a little bit losing its reference on color…

I strongly disagree with referencing Lazurite as any kind of green.
Lazurite Throne sounds cool, but Lazurite is iconic for being very blue.
Azure I believe is the right word here. I can't speak for the intent of the CN text, but it seems to me if it specifically references Lapis or Lazurite, it would be to associate its deep blue-ness with the deep blue-ness of the ocean.
Azure Throne sounds fine if we're trying to go for something close to the CN text, as the word Azure has etymological roots in Lazurite, but if we're picking a mineral, I would describe his throne as being made of **Turquoise**.
I’ll be fine with yellow marks for that
nah that was pretty much it
if it isn't in the gDoc (it isn't), then it's probably not needed
